element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player manages global alien/XCOM craft tracking, base building, hiring staff, resource management
Turn-based isometric tactical combat with squad management; players learn underwater vs surface mechanics
Distinct physics from surface combat; weapon restrictions in aquatic environments force tactical adaptation
Original XCOM tech unusable in salt water; players must develop entirely new weapon/equipment trees
Missions span multiple map sections (upper/lower decks); casualties from earlier parts persist; requires sustained squad viability
New feature added from player feedback; close-range tactical option in underwater engagement
Increased complexity vs predecessor; spatial navigation challenges increase as player advances

typical_relief_triggers: GeoScape phase allowing resource/tech management pause
Transition from combat back to strategic planning
typical_tension_triggers: Multi-part mission stage transitions with persistent casualties
Increased alien activity detected on GeoScape
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Heightened difficulty creates sustained tension; turn-based tactical pacing punctuated by GeoScape management phases; multi-part missions extend engagement duration and psychological pressure

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate initial challenge; underwater mechanics introduce learning curve distinct from predecessor
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Markedly increased difficulty; multi-part missions demand sustained squad management; weapon magazine limitations force strategic restraint
difficulty_curve.late_game: High difficulty; complex map layouts and sustained alien pressure create compounding challenge
notable_spikes: First underwater combat encounter (environmental mechanics shock)
Initial multi-part mission requiring casualty management across phases
Late-game escalation toward T'Leth endgame confrontation

notable_level_patterns.description: Alien terror attacks on seagoing vessels composed of sequential map sections (upper decks → lower decks); all sections must be completed for mission success; casualties from earlier sections do not respawn in later sections
Underwater battles use same physics as ground combat but with environmental restrictions; specific weapons unusable in aquatic contexts; distinct tactical environment from predecessor
Campaign progresses from surface terror attacks to deep-ocean operations, culminating in exploration of T'Leth wreck; spatial progression mirrors narrative escalation
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Gulf of Mexico operations; T'Leth final assault
